reasoning: The unescaped dot in `/polylang\/flags\/(\w+).(?:jpg|png|svg)$/i` at line 143 of includes/functions.php is a genuine regex correctness defect: the dot matches any character instead of a literal period. However, this has zero security impact. (1) The captured group is still constrained to `\w+` characters regardless of the wildcard dot, so no injection through the captured value is possible. (2) The function only returns a flag code string or false — it executes nothing. (3) The input `$flag_url` originates from `pll_the_languages( array('raw'=>1) )` (Polylang's internal API, line 728 of polylang-language-switcher.php), which returns admin-configured language data stored in the database — not attacker-controlled HTTP input. (4) There is no code path by which an unauthenticated or low-privilege user can inject a crafted URL into this regex. The flaw could only cause a false positive match on a URL like `polylang/flags/enXjpg` (any char instead of dot), which would still capture a valid `\w+` code — a harmless mismatch. The claimed reachability ('no direct user trigger') is accurate and confirms the finding is a code quality issue, not a security vulnerability.
